regions.text: Aristotle said everything has a purpose—the work it does best. (Illustrations: Wheel, knife, clock, apple tree, cow).
A good wheel turns well; a good knife cuts well. Likewise, every part of the body has its own work.
What is the true work of a human? Is it to learn? If so, what should we learn? How to act?
Do we learn through feedback? Do we learn by seeing if the outcome of our actions is right?
